0.notes: Code already guards with isEmpty() + create(4,4,1). MidiPlayer in chain 0 (MIDI processors) under the master container.

1.notes: Pure CSS LAF demo with setInlineStyleSheet and div:hover transition. Panel1 must exist for setLocalLookAndFeel to apply.

2.notes: CSS styling with --bgColour and --value CSS variables on sliders. Panel1 acts as a transparent overlay capturing mouse events. Knobs should be stacked vertically. Panel1 must cover same area as knobs.

3.notes: Extends example 2 with CSS :hover pseudo-state management via setStyleSheetPseudoState and Shift+click modal text input via Content.showModalTextInput. Panel1 must be named 'Panel1' (used by parentComponent key in modal config).

4.notes: Engine.createGlobalScriptLookAndFeel registers a global LAF. Without a SliderPack component anywhere in the interface the drawSliderPackBackground callback never fires. Add one programmatically.

5.skipReason: Requires a ScriptFX module ('Script FX1') that contains a compiled scriptnode network with a peak node configured as External Display Buffer #0. Setting up the full scriptnode network (LFO -> peak -> global_cable) programmatically is not feasible for automated testing.
5.notes: Demonstrates Synth.getDisplayBufferSource + ScriptRingBuffer.createPath for 2D signal plotting. The P1 panel is obtained via getComponent — needs a ScriptPanel named 'P1' — but the core blocker is the scriptnode network dependency.

6.notes: Demonstrates exclusive solo-button logic with an effect. The original uses a custom ScriptFX whose script defines .lows/.mids/.highs attributes — those are unavailable without the FX script. Using numeric indices 0/1/2 against SimpleGain is functionally sufficient to demonstrate the toggle pattern.

7.notes: Full 8-band parametric EQ UI with 5 custom LAF functions (drawFilterPath, drawFilterDragHandle, drawAnalyserPath, drawFilterGridLines, drawAnalyserGrid). MainDragFiltFT must be a FloatingTile configured as a FilterDisplay/DraggableFilterPanel type backed by the CurveEq. The control callbacks all call an empty collective_eqWatcher_Function, so none crash. 41 components total.

8.notes: Registers drawAnalyserBackground and drawAnalyserPath LAF functions on a programmatically created FloatingTile plotter. The LFO1 modulator provides the live data stream for the plotter. FloatingTile is created in code (Content.addFloatingTile) so it does not need a component entry.

9.notes: Self-contained 4x4 clickable grid with hover highlighting, all drawn via setPaintRoutine and controlled via setMouseCallback. Panel1.data stores hover index, numCols, numRows.

10.notes: Content.createShader('shader') creates an empty shader object if 'shader.glsl' is not found in the project — the code compiles and runs but renders nothing visible. The uniform setUniformData call is still exercised. Shader file presence is optional for compilation testing.

11.notes: Demonstrates safe knob-linking via setAttribute on effects to avoid infinite callback loops. Two SimpleGain effects in the FX chain (chain 3).

12.notes: Uses Engine.getCpuUsage and Engine.doubleToString inside a panel timer callback to show real-time CPU% via drawAlignedText. Reads panel's own textColour and text properties. startTimer(200) is already in the code.

13.notes: Engine.getGlobalRoutingManager + getCable('Macro1') + connectToGlobalModulator('M1', true) wires the Macro1 cable to receive values from the global modulator named M1. The registered callback sets a SliderPack slider at index 4. A Constant modulator named M1 in the gain chain acts as the source.

14.notes: Demonstrates dynamic knob-to-modulator wiring using index arrays. PITCH_INDEXES defines knob suffixes; PITCH_TARGET_INDEXES maps each knob to a list of Constant modulator indices. Moving Pitch_a sets intensity of Constant1; Pitch_b sets Constant2/3/4; Pitch_c sets Constant5/6.

15.notes: Simple g.rotate demo using Math.toRadians(-90) around panel center to draw vertically oriented text.

16.notes: drawLinearSlider LAF only fires when the slider style is a linear variant (not rotary). Knob1 must have its style changed to 'Linear Horizontal' (or 'Linear Vertical') to trigger the registered LAF function.

17.notes: Uses obj.valueAsText in drawRotarySlider to overlay the current formatted value as centred text. Knobs use default rotary style. Note: code references Colours.whitesmoke — verify HISE supports this colour constant (standard CSS colour name).

18.notes: Registers drawMatrixPeakMeter LAF that fills an SVG icon path with opacity driven by obj.peaks[0]. The icon path data is hard-coded via Content.createPath + loadFromData. FloatingTile1 must be configured as a MatrixPeakMeter to supply the obj.peaks data.

19.notes: Uses Engine.createTransportHandler with setEnableGrid, setSyncToMasterClock, setSyncMode(InternalOnly), and setOnGridChange to toggle a panel text on each grid beat. Two MidiPlayers in the MIDI processor chain (chain 0).

20.notes: Content.createShader('bunny.glsl') creates an empty shader object if the file is missing — code compiles and runs but renders a blank panel. The getCurrentLevel + uniform smoothing logic is still exercised. Panel is created in code (Content.addPanel). SineWaveGenerator1 provides getCurrentLevel data.

21.notes: Custom tab LAF using Content.createPath with quadraticTo for rounded-corner tabs. Active tab draws a notch at the bottom; inactive tab is flat. Clicking toggles the paired panel visibility. Buttons should be set to toggle mode (radioGroup or individual toggle).

22.notes: Uses Content.getAllComponents('Knob\d') and Content.getAllComponents('ComboBox\d') to build paired arrays. The LAF reads obj.id to find the index, then reads the paired ComboBox value to pick a fill color. Needs at least one Knob and one ComboBox with IDs matching the regex patterns. ComboBox must have 3 items (for states 0/1/2). Sliders should be in linear mode so drawLinearSlider fires.

23.notes: Engine.createBroadcaster with attachToComponentMouseEvents and addListener to log hover events. Uses Content.getAllComponents('Button\d') so any button with an ID matching the pattern works. At least one Button needed.

24.notes: Demonstrates JSON object as lookup table keyed by getItemText() result. ComboBox1 must have items 'Animal 1' through 'Animal 10' so getItemText() returns keys that match the animals object.
